---
title: "My very interesting report"
format: html
author: Your name
date: xxxx-xx-xx
---

```{r}
library(__________)
# Download and read in the data etc ...
```

# Introduction

Introduce the question you want to investigate

## Why this question matters  

Motivate briefly ...
At first glance, this research question may look trivial — the kind of thing you’d ask over a coffee break rather than in a scientific paper. But that’s exactly why it’s interesting: if something is so “obvious,” shouldn’t we already know the answer? Spoiler: we don’t.  

Exploring this topic allows us to bridge the gap between common sense and actual evidence. It matters because it touches everyday life, yet the implications could be surprisingly wide — from policy decisions to whether we get bragging rights at the next lab meeting. In short: it’s important, it’s underexplored, and let’s be honest… it’s also fun to investigate.  

## Data and methods

Describe step by step what you are doing and why. It does not need to be super polished. Be honest about the limitations of the data and the methods you are using.

# Results

Present your results. Use graphs, tables etc ... whatever you find relevant!

# Discussion

How do you interpret the results? What are the implications of the findings? What could be done differently to obtain more reliable results? Do you suggest any further research?
